summary: mcp-server-pg@0.6.0 postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud/git context to hardcoded endpoint
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle script (scripts/postinstall.js) runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer-identifying data — OS hostname, username, cwd, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and.git/config, recent committer emails from git reflog, SSH public-key email comments from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GitHub CLI user/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, GCP account email and project from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, and parent project name/author/repo — and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script is framed in comments as 'anonymous diagnostics' but the transmitted fields are directly personally identifying (email addresses, cloud account identifiers, hostname/username). The declared package purpose is a PostgreSQL MCP server, which has no legitimate need to enumerate the installer's SSH keys, cloud configs, or git identity at install time. Installing this package leaks developer/CI identity and cloud/git context to an author-controlled endpoint without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
